free_text: Excitation functions and yields of (d,a) reaction on
natural molybdenum up to the deuteron energy of 13 MeV
Inst. of Mineral Raw Materials, Kutna Hora
Experimental details
U-120 cyclotron
Lapf
Some corrections included by CAJaD
Reaction lines are corrected.
Last checking has been done.
On. 006-008 moved to D0822. 009-012 added.
Stack 1
Stack 2
Mo foils (17.82 mg/cm2 thick, >99% purity) and Al
foils (3.02 mg/cm2 thick).
Mo foils (20.37 mg/cm2 thick)
Resolution 2.6 KeV (FWHM) At 1332.5 KeV
Energy loss was calculated by range and
mass stopping power table by Williamson (1962).
Faraday cup used in parallel with calorimetric
determination and by means of monitor reaction
17.78 hr irradiation, E0=13.5 MeV, 3.4 uA
5.0 min irradiation, E0=12.0 MeV, 5.3 uA
Range and mass stopping power table given
Relative accuracy of cross section determination
better than 10% except at highest and low energies
where errors can amount to as much as 40%.
Table 2 of Appl.Radiat.Isotopes 28(1977)555
On. Thick target yields moved to 009;
REACTION(SF4-SF5): -G,M+ deleted.
Stack 1
Stack 2
Mo foils (17.82 mg/cm2 thick, >99% purity) and Al
foils (3.02 mg/cm2 thick).
Mo foils (20.37 mg/cm2 thick)
Resolution 2.6 KeV (FWHM) At 1332.5 KeV
Energy loss was calculated by range and
mass stopping power table by Williamson (1962).
Faraday cup used in parallel with calorimetric
determination and by means of monitor reaction
17.78 hr irradiation, E0=13.5 MeV, 3.4 uA
5.0 min irradiation, E0=12.0 MeV, 5.3 uA
Range and mass stopping power table given
Contribution of (n,p) reaction was determined by
activity of Mo foils placed beyond the deuteron
range.
By KaChaPaG:
Above ~7 MeV the 95Mo(d,a+n)92Nb even with larger
abundance contributes increasingly. The values given
in the DATA section have to be recorrected for the
natural target.
Relative accuracy of cross section determination
better than 10% except at highest and low energies
where errors can amount to as much as 40%.
Table 2 of Appl.Radiat.Isotopes 28(1977)555
On. Thick target yields moved to 009.
REACTION: SF1 changed from 42-MO-0, SF8=FCT deleted.
Mo foils (17.82 mg/cm2 thick, >99% purity) and Al
foils (3.02 mg/cm2 thick).
Resolution 2.6 KeV (FWHM) At 1332.5 KeV
Energy loss was calculated by range and
mass stopping power table by Williamson (1962).
Faraday cup used in parallel with calorimetric
determination and by means of monitor reaction
17.78 hr irradiation, E0=13.5 MeV, 3.4 uA
Range and mass stopping power table given
Contribution of (n,p) reaction was determined by
activity of Mo foils placed beyond the deuteron
range.
Contribution of 95mNb production subtracted from the
95gNb production cross section (amounts to ~20% of
the primarily produced 95gNb).
By KaChaPaG:
Above ~7 MeV the 98Mo(d,a+n)95Nb even with larger
abundance contributes increasingly. The values given
in the DATA section have to be recorrected for the
natural target.
Relative accuracy of cross section determination
better than 10% except at highest and low energies
where errors can amount to as much as 40%.

Integration of excitation function
Because R/Y=ln(2)/T in Eqs.(2-3) and R is introduced
as the "saturation product rate", the Y ("thick
target yield") was coded as the physical yield.
